AccountUpdateRequest.description:
PATCH-style. ``code`` and ``type`` are intentionally NOT here — the
service rejects them via a separate guard if a client sneaks them in.
AccountUpdateRequest.title: AccountUpdateRequest

AccountingPeriodUpdate.description: Only the display name is mutable post-create. Dates are immutable.
AccountingPeriodUpdate.title: AccountingPeriodUpdate

ApprovalActionRequest.description: Used for approve / reject. ``decision_note`` is optional free text.
ApprovalActionRequest.title: ApprovalActionRequest

ApprovalCancelRequest.description: Cancel uses ``reason`` for surface symmetry; stored as decision_note.
ApprovalCancelRequest.title: ApprovalCancelRequest

AuditLogResponse.description:
Paginated list response. ``next_cursor`` is an opaque base64 token
that, when passed back, resumes pagination just past the last returned
row. ``null`` when the list is exhausted.
AuditLogResponse.title: AuditLogResponse

AuditLogRow.description: One row in the audit_log read model, as returned by the query API.
AuditLogRow.title: AuditLogRow

BillItemCreate.description:
One line on a draft bill. The service computes ``extended_amount``
from ``quantity * unit_price``.
BillItemCreate.title: BillItemCreate

BillStateTransitionRequest.description: Reserved for any future per-transition payload (note, reason).
BillStateTransitionRequest.title: BillStateTransitionRequest

BillUpdate.description:
PATCH-style — only fields the caller wants to change.

``items`` replaces the entire line set when supplied. Only legal
while the bill is in ``draft``.
BillUpdate.title: BillUpdate

BillableSourceRef.description:
Per-line ``billable_source`` reference on an invoice line.

When set on an ``InvoiceItemCreate``, the invoice composer loads the
referenced source, validates customer + billable + not-yet-billed,
applies the markup, and stamps ``billed_invoice_item_id`` on the
source row in the same transaction.
BillableSourceRef.title: BillableSourceRef

BulkSettingUpdateRequest.description:
POST body for a batch update.

The request is all-or-nothing: if any value fails validation, no
writes happen.
BulkSettingUpdateRequest.title: BulkSettingUpdateRequest

BulkSettingUpdateResponse.description:
Response for a successful batch update.

``updated`` mirrors the request keys with their validated (typed)
values as returned by the service.
BulkSettingUpdateResponse.title: BulkSettingUpdateResponse

CalcPlateInputPayload.description: A single plate's shape as proposed by the composer.
CalcPlateInputPayload.title: CalcPlateInputPayload

CalcRequest.description: POST body. Exactly one of ``job_id`` or ``inputs`` must be set.
CalcRequest.title: CalcRequest

CameraConfigRequest.description:
Used for both POST (set/replace) and the body of PUT/PATCH-style
upserts. ``password_secret`` is write-only — never echoed back.
CameraConfigRequest.title: CameraConfigRequest

CustomFieldUpdateRequest.description:
PATCH — fields the user wants to change.

``entity_type``, ``key``, and ``field_type`` are intentionally
immutable: changing them would break stored data on existing rows.
CustomFieldUpdateRequest.title: CustomFieldUpdateRequest

CustomerAddress.description:
Snapshot-friendly address shape for billing + shipping.

All fields are optional so callers can stage a partially complete
address; the service stores the object as-is.
CustomerAddress.title: CustomerAddress

DiscoveredPlateResponse.description: Parser output. No DB writes — the UI uses this to pre-fill a plate form.
DiscoveredPlateResponse.title: DiscoveredPlateResponse

DivisionUpdateRequest.description:
PATCH-style — only ``name`` is editable. The router rejects any
``code`` field (immutable post-create).
DivisionUpdateRequest.title: DivisionUpdateRequest

ExpenseClaimLineCreate.description: One line on a draft expense claim.
ExpenseClaimLineCreate.title: ExpenseClaimLineCreate

ExpenseClaimLineUpdate.description: PATCH-style — only fields the caller wants to change on a single line.
ExpenseClaimLineUpdate.title: ExpenseClaimLineUpdate

ExpenseClaimSubmitResponse.description:
Wraps the claim + an optional approval_request_id when the
threshold gating attached one.
ExpenseClaimSubmitResponse.title: ExpenseClaimSubmitResponse

FixedAssetUpdate.description:
Metadata-only patch.

Cost/life/depreciation method changes are blocked at the service
layer once any depreciation has been posted (relevant once Phase
9.3 lands).
FixedAssetUpdate.title: FixedAssetUpdate

InventoryLocationUpdateRequest.description: PATCH-style — only fields the user wants to change.
InventoryLocationUpdateRequest.title: InventoryLocationUpdateRequest

InventoryTransactionCreate.description:
Body for ``POST /api/v1/inventory/transactions``.

``quantity`` is the positive magnitude for every kind except
``adjustment``, where the caller supplies a signed delta.
InventoryTransactionCreate.title: InventoryTransactionCreate

InventoryTransferResponse.description: Response from the transfer endpoint — returns both halves.
InventoryTransferResponse.title: InventoryTransferResponse

InvoiceItemCreate.description:
One line on a draft invoice. The service computes
``extended_amount`` from ``quantity * unit_price``.
InvoiceItemCreate.title: InvoiceItemCreate

InvoiceStateTransitionRequest.description: Reserved for any future per-transition payload (note, reason).
InvoiceStateTransitionRequest.title: InvoiceStateTransitionRequest

InvoiceUpdate.description:
PATCH-style — only fields the caller wants to change.

``items`` replaces the entire line set when supplied. Only legal
while the invoice is in ``draft``.
InvoiceUpdate.title: InvoiceUpdate

JobUpdate.description:
Only ``priority``, ``due_at``, ``notes`` are editable post-create.

``product_id`` and ``quantity_ordered`` are immutable; the service
rejects them with 400.
JobUpdate.title: JobUpdate

JournalEntryPendingApprovalResponse.description:
Returned from POST /accounting/entries with HTTP 202 when an entry
crosses the configured approval threshold.
JournalEntryPendingApprovalResponse.title: JournalEntryPendingApprovalResponse

MaterialDetailResponse.description: Material + the most recent 10 receipts (newest first).
MaterialDetailResponse.title: MaterialDetailResponse

MaterialUpdateRequest.description: PATCH-style — only fields the user wants to change.
MaterialUpdateRequest.title: MaterialUpdateRequest

OnHandSummaryResponse.description: Aggregated across locations for a single entity.
OnHandSummaryResponse.title: OnHandSummaryResponse

ParentChainItem.description:
Leaner shape for the parent chain — avoids recursive parent_chain
blowup on the wire.
ParentChainItem.title: ParentChainItem

PrinterResponse.description:
Printer response. Excludes ``moonraker_api_key`` by construction —
the boolean ``moonraker_api_key_set`` is the only signal we surface
so the UI can decide whether to show "configured" vs "not set".
PrinterResponse.title: PrinterResponse

PrinterStateResponse.description: Live state snapshot for a single printer.
PrinterStateResponse.title: PrinterStateResponse

PrinterUpdateRequest.description:
PATCH-style — only fields the user wants to change.

Passing ``moonraker_api_key: null`` clears the stored secret; passing
a non-null string replaces it.
PrinterUpdateRequest.title: PrinterUpdateRequest

ProductUpdateRequest.description: PATCH-style: only fields the caller wants to change.
ProductUpdateRequest.title: ProductUpdateRequest

QuoteItemCreate.description:
One line on a draft quote. The service computes
``extended_amount`` from ``quantity * unit_price``.
QuoteItemCreate.title: QuoteItemCreate

QuoteStateTransitionRequest.description: Reserved for any future per-transition payload (note, reason).
QuoteStateTransitionRequest.title: QuoteStateTransitionRequest

QuoteUpdate.description:
PATCH-style — only fields the caller wants to change.

``items`` replaces the entire line set when supplied. Only legal
while the quote is in ``draft``.
QuoteUpdate.title: QuoteUpdate

RateUpdateRequest.description:
PATCH-style — only fields the user wants to change.

``is_default_for_kind`` is intentionally not editable here; use the
dedicated ``set-default`` endpoint to flip it atomically.
RateUpdateRequest.title: RateUpdateRequest

ReferenceSequenceRow.description: A single ``reference_sequence`` row, surfaced via the admin API.
ReferenceSequenceRow.title: ReferenceSequenceRow

RefundApprovalDecision.description:
Body for approve / reject. ``note`` becomes the decision_note on
the linked ApprovalRequest if one was created.
RefundApprovalDecision.title: RefundApprovalDecision

RefundCreateResponse.description:
Wraps the refund + an optional approval_request_id for the 202
pending-approval case. The router decides 201 vs 202 by inspecting
state == pending_approval.
RefundCreateResponse.title: RefundCreateResponse

RefundItemCreate.description:
One line on a draft refund.

``unit_amount`` is the refund value per unit (typically the
originating ``sale_item.unit_price``; the service does not enforce
that — partial-price refunds are allowed). ``extended_amount`` is
computed by the service as ``quantity * unit_amount``.
RefundItemCreate.title: RefundItemCreate

Role.description:
RBAC roles. Deny-by-default; presence in a `require_role` allowlist
is the only thing that grants access.
Role.title: Role

SaleCogsConsumptionResponse.description: One slice taken from a single FIFO lot.
SaleCogsConsumptionResponse.title: SaleCogsConsumptionResponse

SaleItemCreate.description:
One line on a draft sale. The service computes ``extended_amount``
from ``quantity * unit_price`` — the caller doesn't supply it.

``description`` and ``sku_or_job_number`` are snapshotted onto the
sale_item row at create/update time so later catalog edits don't
rewrite history.
SaleItemCreate.title: SaleItemCreate

SaleStateTransitionRequest.description:
Reserved for any future per-transition payload (note, reason).

Phase 6.2 ships with zero-body state transitions — the endpoint paths
encode the target state — but the schema is wired so Phase 6.3 can
extend it without breaking codegen.
SaleStateTransitionRequest.title: SaleStateTransitionRequest

SaleUpdate.description:
PATCH-style — only fields the caller wants to change.

``items`` replaces the entire line set when supplied (simplest model
for a draft). Omit to keep existing lines.
SaleUpdate.title: SaleUpdate

SalesChannelUpdate.description: PATCH-style — only fields the caller wants to change.
SalesChannelUpdate.title: SalesChannelUpdate

SettingResponse.description: One setting, merged with its schema default and provenance.

schema_type.description: Friendly Python type name for the value (e.g. 'Decimal', 'str', 'dict'). Used by the frontend to pick an editor.
schema_type.title: Schema Type

updated_at.description: When the row was last written. None if no row exists yet.
updated_at.title: Updated At

updated_by_user_id.description: User who last wrote the row. None if from default.
updated_by_user_id.title: Updated By User Id

SettingResponse.title: SettingResponse

SettingUpdateRequest.description: PUT body for a single setting.
SettingUpdateRequest.title: SettingUpdateRequest

ShipmentTransitionRequest.description:
Body for the shipment state-transition endpoints — empty today,
declared so callers can attach future fields (notes, override flags)
without breaking the contract.
ShipmentTransitionRequest.title: ShipmentTransitionRequest

SupplyUpdateRequest.description: PATCH-style — only fields the user wants to change.
SupplyUpdateRequest.title: SupplyUpdateRequest

UserCreateResponse.description:
Same as UserResponse but carries the freshly-generated password.

The password appears in the response body exactly once — there is no
other surface that returns it. The router never logs it.
UserCreateResponse.title: UserCreateResponse

UserResponse.description: Single user, as returned by GET /users and PATCH /users/{id}.
UserResponse.title: UserResponse

VendorAddress.description: Snapshot-friendly address shape for billing + shipping.
VendorAddress.title: VendorAddress

VerifyChainResponse.description:
Result of ``GET /admin/events/verify-chain``.

``ok=True`` means every event's recomputed hash matches what we stored
and every ``prev_event_hash`` matches the previous row's
``event_hash``. ``broken_at_position`` is the first position where
the chain fails.

broken_at_position.description: First position where verification failed (None if ok=True).
broken_at_position.title: Broken At Position

last_position.description: Last position the verifier successfully validated.
last_position.title: Last Position

VerifyChainResponse.title: VerifyChainResponse

get.description:
Return per-account balances.

A row with no posted activity yet still appears here with zero
totals (we left-join Account → AccountBalance). Single-account
lookup via ``?account_id=`` always returns one row, even if no
activity has accumulated.

schema.title: Response Post Entry Api V1 Accounting Entries Post

post.description:
Dispatch a previously approved large-journal-entry approval into a
posted entry.

Preserves the original requester as ``actor_user_id`` so audit /
balance attribution stays accurate. The post call sets
``_internal_skip_approval_check=True`` — the request already passed
the gate when it was approved.

get.description: Stream the audit log as CSV. Same filters as the JSON endpoint.

Walk the event log in ``position`` order and recompute every hash.

Window-bounded: ``to_position - from_position`` may not exceed 1M.
Returns ``ok=true`` for a clean chain, otherwise ``ok=false`` with
the position where the chain first breaks.

Return every ``(prefix, year, last_value)`` row, sorted by
``(prefix, year)``.

schema.title: Response List Reference Sequences Api V1 Admin Reference Sequences Get

get.description:
List approval requests.

Admin roles (owner, bookkeeper) see all rows. Other roles see only
their own requests — the service scopes the query by
``requested_by_user_id``.

post.description:
Cancel a pending request.

Allowed for the original requester or an owner. The endpoint passes
``actor_is_owner`` to the service rather than letting the service
re-query user roles.

get.description:
Return entities whose ``total_on_hand < low_stock_threshold``.

Entities with no configured threshold never appear. Sorted by
deficit descending. All authenticated roles can read this — the
alert surface needs broad visibility.

Return ``inventory_on_hand`` rows, optionally filtered.

Default shape: a list of per-(entity, location) rows AND a list of
per-entity summaries (total + per-location map). The caller can use
either depending on whether they need the breakdown or the rollup.

post.description:
Parse a PrusaSlicer/Bambu Studio .gcode.json sidecar and return the
extracted plate fields. No DB writes — the UI uses this to pre-fill
the plate-create form. Any authenticated role may call this.
Operator-triggered sweep: mark overdue, then apply fees.

Convert an accepted quote into an invoice (Phase 7.3, #111).

Returns ``{"invoice_id": "..."}`` and a 201 status. The quote must
be in state ``accepted`` and not yet have an invoice attached.

get.description: Return the FIFO COGS breakdown for ``sale_id`` (no writes).
Return every registered setting, merged with schema defaults.

schema.title: Response List Settings Api V1 Settings Get

get.description: Return a single setting by key. 400 on unknown key.

put.description: Validate and persist a single setting. Emits SettingChanged.

post.description: Atomic batch update. One invalid value rolls back everything.

get.description:
Stream the rendered label PDF.

Local-FS backend today returns the bytes directly. Once the S3
backend lands, this handler will issue a signed URL redirect
instead — the caller-facing contract stays a single GET that
delivers the PDF.
